articles.title: Three Is Company, Two’s a Trial: Reference Counts in Federal Circuit Obviousness Appeals
articles.content:
作者：Dennis Crouch

我最近的博文测量了USPTO审查意见中的现有技术显而易见性组合。Dennis Crouch，《三人成虎：§103引用数量的新数据》，Patently-O（2026年6月）。本文则从法院视角提出相同问题。我收集了2013年至2026年间复审地区法院显而易见性裁决的联邦巡回法院判决，共发现约项涉及现有技术的判决。正如预期，法院案件引用的现有技术参考文献更少。

正如我在另一篇近期文章中所述，法官审判（由法官裁决）中，参考文献组合平均为2.7个，约十分之一达到五个或更多。另一方面，陪审团审判平均仅2.0个参考文献，53%恰好使用两个参考文献，且无一超过四个。参见Dennis Crouch，《借鉴委员会：后IPR时代的地区法院显而易见性》，Patently-O（2026年5月21日）。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

My recent post measured prior art obviousness combinations from USPTO office actions. Dennis Crouch, Three Is Company: New Data on § 103 Reference Counts, Patently-O (June 2026). This post asks the same question in the courts. I collected the Federal Circuit decisions from 2013 to 2026 that review a district court obviousness ruling on the merits. I found about decisions reviewing obviousness that name the prior art.  As you might expect, the court cases rely upon fewer prior art references.

As I suggested in another recent post, bench trials (where the judge is deciding the case) average 2.7 references as the combination, and about one in ten reaches five or more references. On the other hand, jury trials average only 2.0 references with 53% resting on exactly two references and none exceeding four. See, Dennis Crouch, Borrowing from the Board: District Court Obviousness in the Post-IPR Era, Patently-O (May 21, 2026).

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
